commit.message:
fix: correct AMS HT tray_id encoding in set_ams_filament_setting

AMS HT chip_id=128. Standard AMS tray_id = ams_id * 4 + slot, but
AMS HT tray_id = chip_id + slot (128 + slot). Previously produced 512
for slot 0 instead of the correct 128.

commit.message:
Fix: use next/server after() for Slack interaction background work

Fire-and-forget (.catch) doesn't work on Vercel — the serverless
function is killed immediately after the response, so the rating
was never saved, the message never updated, and the next item
never dispatched. Using after() keeps the function alive to
complete the work after returning 200 to Slack.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: support multi-race import from Google Sheets (#101)

* feat(sheets-api): add multi-sheet support for reading specific sheets

- Add readRangeFromSheet() to read from specific sheet by name
- Add getSheetNames() to list all sheets in a spreadsheet
- Add filterRaceSheets() to extract race tabs (exclude 'Overall')
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing readRange()


* feat(scorecard): add multi-race parsing support

- Add parseMultipleRaces() to parse Race 1, Race 2 tabs separately
- Returns list of ImportPreview objects, one per race sheet
- Skips missing sheets with logging
- Maintains existing single-race parse() for backward compatibility


* feat(import): support multi-race import from Google Sheets

- Update previewSheet() to detect and parse all race tabs (Race 1, Race 2, etc.)
- Filter out 'Overall' tab and other non-race sheets
- previewSheet returns list of previews with race sheet names and isMultiRace flag
- execute() now processes multiple races from single Google Sheet
- Driver matches confirmed across all races (consistent assignments)
- Cumulative import result aggregates all races


* style(template): update import-preview for multi-race display

- Add multi-race preview rendering with separate sections per race
- Display race sheet names when multi-race import
- Maintain backward compatibility with single preview (CSV)
- Improve UX for multiple races: show race names, separate error handling


* fix(import): reuse matches for multi-race imports

- Refactored CsvImportService to process all race previews together via executeMultiRaceImport()
- Changed CsvImportController to call executeMultiRaceImport() once instead of looping per preview
- This ensures one Match is created for each team pair with multiple Races (legs) instead of multiple Matches
- Added repository methods: findFirstByMatchdayIdAndHomeTeamIdAndAwayTeamId() and findByMatchId()
- Updated test mocks to use new repository methods

Fixes the bug where importing 2 races for the same team pair would attempt to create 2 Match entities.


* test(import): add comprehensive multi-race import unit tests

- Added 5 new tests for executeMultiRaceImport() method:
  1. givenMultipleRacePreviewsForSameTeamPair_whenExecuteMultiRaceImport_thenCreatesOneMatchWithMultipleRaces
  2. givenMultipleRacePreviewsWithDifferentTeamPairs_whenExecuteMultiRaceImport_thenCreatesMultipleMatches
  3. givenMultipleRacePreviewsSameTeamPair_whenExecuteMultiRaceImportWithOverwrite_thenDeletesExistingRaces
  4. givenMultipleRacePreviewsSameTeamPairWithoutOverwrite_whenExecuteMultiRaceImport_thenAddsError
  5. givenMultipleRacePreviewsMixedTeamPairs_whenExecuteMultiRaceImport_thenHandlesMixedScenario

Tests verify the core behavior: reusing matches for same team pairs across multiple race legs


* fix(import): prevent driver duplicate key constraint violation

When importing drivers with createNewDrivers flag, check if the driver already
exists before attempting to create a new one. This prevents unique constraint
violations on PSN_ID when the same driver is imported multiple times or already
exists in the database.

- Added check for existing driver before insertion in resolveDriver()
- Returns existing driver if found
- Only creates new driver if PSN_ID doesn't exist
- Fixes error: Unique index or primary key violation on DRIVERS(PSN_ID)

* fix(import): defensive sheet filtering for single-tab imports

Make sheet filtering logic more defensive to handle single-tab Google Sheets
with arbitrary tab names (not starting with 'Race').

Changes:
- Modified filterRaceSheets() to use fallback strategy:
  1. First try to find sheets with 'Race' in the name (multi-race scenario)
  2. If none found, fall back to any sheet except 'Overall' (single-tab scenario)
- This allows imports to work regardless of tab naming conventions
- Updated error message to reflect more generic sheet handling
- Added tests:
  * givenSheetNamesWithoutRace_whenFilterRaceSheets_thenReturnsFallbackSheet
  * givenOnlyOverallSheet_whenFilterRaceSheets_thenReturnsEmpty
  * givenSingleTabWithArbitraryName_whenFilterRaceSheets_thenReturnsFallback


* test(dataimport): add coverage tests for CSV and Sheets import

- Add 13 new unit tests to CsvImportServiceTest covering uncovered code paths:
  * Empty previews list handling
  * Team name normalization (spaces ↔ underscores)
  * CSV header skipping with 'team' and 'psn' keywords
  * Invalid position and quali values
  * Boolean parsing variations (true, ja, x, ✓, etc.)
  * SeasonDriver team updates
  * Existing driver reuse
  * Team pair grouping with single team
  * Playoff matchup not found error
  * Team not found in season teams
  * Matchday not found error

- Add 8 new unit tests to GoogleSheetsServiceTest covering:
  * Empty sheet names list
  * Only Overall sheet scenario
  * Multiple Overall sheet variations
  * Mixed race and overall sheets
  * Wildcard sheet name patterns
  * Null and blank credentials paths
  * Special characters in spreadsheet IDs
  * URL query parameters handling

- Improve instruction coverage for org.ctc.dataimport from 71% to 73%
- Improve branch coverage for org.ctc.dataimport from 67% to 70%

* fix: Aggregate multi-leg match scores correctly on CSV import

When importing race results via CSV, the overall match scores are now calculated
by aggregating all legs from the database instead of relying on potentially
incomplete lazy-loaded collections. This ensures that when Leg 2 results are
imported, scores from both Leg 1 and Leg 2 are included.

Changes:
- ScoringService.aggregateMatchScores() now uses findByMatchId() and
  findByPlayoffMatchupId() to load all legs from the database
- Fixes bug where multi-leg matches showed only the last leg's score
- Also updates quickScore() to call aggregateMatchScores() for consistency

* fix: Display individual race scores instead of aggregated match scores in race list

The race list page now shows scores calculated from individual RaceResults for each race,
not the aggregated Match scores. This allows multi-leg matches to show:
- Leg 1: 30:40
- Leg 2: 29:17

Instead of showing the aggregated 59:57 on both rows.

Changes:
- getRaceListData() now calculates race scores from RaceResults
- Falls back to match scores only when no RaceResults exist
- Ensures race list shows individual leg results while matchday/match views show aggregates

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Harden Colab startup and tunnel flow

Make Colab startup wait for actual HTTP readiness, always rebuild frontend after pull, and make Cloudflare tunnel setup more robust (127.0.0.1 target, no-autoupdate, stale process cleanup, broader URL parsing, and reachability check).

feat: add Phase 2 deliverables — Harbor and Centralised Logging

Create all Phase 2 documentation and IaC for Harbor container registry
and centralised logging (Loki + Promtail + Grafana).

New files:
- docs/deployment/harbor/README.md: Full Harbor LXC deployment guide
  covering Proxmox LXC provisioning, FIPS/STIG baseline, NFS storage
  mount, Harbor via Podman Compose, Step-CA TLS, Keycloak OIDC, project
  RBAC (PRD 17.3), Trivy CVE policy, Cosign/Notary v2 signing enforcement,
  SBOM generation, Promtail agent, and backup configuration.
- docs/deployment/loki-promtail/README.md: Full Loki + Promtail + Grafana
  deployment guide covering NFS log share provisioning with restrictive
  ACLs, Loki LXC build, Loki single-process mode config, Step-CA TLS,
  Keycloak OIDC access control (platform:devops denied), Grafana LXC
  (DP-004 Option A), Promtail reference deployment, NFS mount verification,
  and retention verification.
- gitops/infrastructure/harbor/promtail-config.yaml: Promtail config for
  Harbor LXC — ships core API, registry access, audit, job service, Trivy
  scanner, OS security, and systemd journal logs to Loki.
- gitops/infrastructure/loki/loki-config.yaml: Loki single-process config
  with auth_enabled, TSDB schema, NFS filesystem backend, 12-month
  retention (8784h), compactor enforcement, and TLS 1.3 minimum.
- docs/phase-2/harbor-logging-verification.md: Phase 2 verification
  checklist (H-01..H-25, L-01..L-18, G-01..G-04, E-01..E-05) mapping
  all AC-18.3 acceptance criteria to evidence files.
- docs/phase-2/evidence/.gitkeep: Placeholder for evidence directory.

Modified files:
- argocd_process.md: Phase 2 status updated from NOT STARTED to
  IN PROGRESS; full Tasks table with Owner/Evidence columns; EA
  Deliverables (COMPLETE) table; Acceptance Criteria Tracking table;
  Issues and Deviations table; footer updated.
- README.md: Duplicate content removed (second Overview..Classification
  block); Status header updated to Phase 2; Phase 2 status table added;
  repository structure updated to reflect Phase 2 deliverables.

commit.message:
Ⓜ feat: add configurable reply guard for message edits (#39)

Add a guardrail that detects when editing a message that already has
replies. Supports three modes via TEAMS_EDIT_REPLY_GUARD env var or
per-call replyGuard parameter:

- allow (default): edit proceeds normally (backward-compatible)
- warn: edit proceeds with annotation appended
- block: edit refused with error

Implements reply detection by fetching thread messages and filtering
out root message and deleted messages.

Closes #26

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update payment.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Simplify database: drop songs table, serve from constants by ESC year, store country codes in votes (#25)

Songs are static per ESC year and don't belong in the database. Votes
stored English country names, making them fragile to typos. All old data
is eradicated (not yet in production).

### Schema changes
- Migration 006: deletes all existing data, drops `songs` table
- Remaining tables: `games` (with `escYear`), `voters`, `votes`
- `votes.country` now stores ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 codes (e.g. `"MDA"`)
instead of `"Moldova"`

### Constants restructure
- `ESC_SONGS` → `ESC_SONGS_BY_YEAR` with `as const satisfies
Record<number, readonly UnsafeSong[]>` to preserve literal types, plus
`code` field on each entry
- `CountryCode` union type derived from the data: `(typeof
ESC_SONGS_BY_YEAR)[keyof typeof ESC_SONGS_BY_YEAR][number]["code"]`
- `Song` interface has `code: CountryCode` — the strongly typed default
used by all consumers
- `UnsafeSong` is a private interface with `code: string`, used only for
the `satisfies` constraint (avoids circular dependency with
`CountryCode`)
- `getSongsForYear(year)` helper replaces DB queries, returns `readonly
Song[]` so `CountryCode` flows naturally to all consumers without type
casts
- `Song` interface: removed `id` and `runningOrder` (no longer DB rows)

### Data layer
- `createGame`: no longer inserts 35 song rows in batches
- `deleteGame`: no longer deletes from `songs`
- `getAssumedVotesForVoter` / `getResultsByScore`: take `escYear` param,
match votes by `song.code`

### UI components
- Pages fetch songs via `getSongsForYear(game.escYear)` instead of
`getSongs(gameId)`
- `RatingButtons` accepts `countryCode` prop typed as `CountryCode` for
vote persistence, `country` for display
- `SongInfo.code` in `DashboardControls` is strictly typed as
`CountryCode` union literal
- `VoteSongList.songs` uses `Song` so `CountryCode` propagates without
casts
- Vote lookups key on `song.code` instead of `song.country`

### Testing
- Added unit tests for `getSongsForYear`, song data integrity (unique
codes/names, required fields, 3-letter ISO format), and `CountryCode`
type safety
- Added `unit` job to CI workflow to run `pnpm run test:unit` on every
push/PR

```ts
// Before: songs duplicated into DB per game
const songs = await getSongs(game.id);
voteRecord[v.country]   // "Moldova"

// After: songs from constants, votes keyed by code
const songs = getSongsForYear(game.escYear);
voteRecord[v.country]   // "MDA"
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: valscion <482561+valscion@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: remove plain_text_input from message (unsupported by Slack)

plain_text_input is only supported in modals/home tabs, not in messages.
This caused chat.postMessage to fail with an invalid blocks error.
Reverted to rating-buttons-only in the message. Added error logging
to sendSlackDM for future debugging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: fix all open Dependabot + CodeQL alerts

Dependabot fixes:
- waitress: 2.1.2 → 3.0.0 (Docker/3.8), 3.0.2 (host/3.10)
  Fixes CRITICAL CVE (race condition) and HIGH CVE (DoS)
  Note: Docker 3.0.0 is max for Python 3.8, health server is
  internal-only so residual risk is minimal
- requests: 2.31.0 → 2.32.4 (Docker/3.8), 2.33.0 (host/3.10)
  Fixes .netrc leak, verify=False, temp file reuse CVEs
- flask: 3.0.3 (Docker/3.8), 3.1.3 (host/3.10)
  Fixes Vary: Cookie header CVE

CodeQL fixes:
- provision.py: Mask hostname and device_id in log output
  to avoid clear-text logging of sensitive data

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Task 6: Map editor - Place Light tool

Add light placement and editing to the C# WinForms map editor:

- Models.cs: Add LightData class with all light properties (name, type,
  position, z-height, color, intensity, radius, spot-only direction/cone).
  Add Lights list to MapData for JSON serialization.

- MapCanvas.cs: Add DrawLight enum value and Light selectable type.
  Implement light rendering with sun icon, radiating rays, and translucent
  colored radius circle. Spot lights show direction arrow and dash-dot
  radius. Selected lights show four cardinal grab handles for interactive
  radius adjustment. Full hit-testing, selection, drag-to-move, and
  radius-drag support. Delete key removes selected lights. Inactive maps
  show lights as dimmed circles.

- MainForm.cs: Add 'Place Light [L]' toolbar button and keyboard shortcut.
  Add light property panel in left sidebar with fields for name, type
  (point/spot dropdown), position, Z height, RGB color (0-1 floats) with
  live color swatch, intensity, radius, and spot-only direction/cone
  angle fields. Light count shown in status bar. Serialization handles
  empty Lights collection (omitted from JSON when empty).

- Update architecture.md with Place Light tool documentation.
- Move completed task to CompletedTasks.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: publish secure terminal P2P chat project

Ship a complete terminal-based peer-to-peer chat application with plaintext and encrypted messaging modes.

Includes modular runtime/crypto/protocol/UI components, cross-platform behavior, and comprehensive README + Report documentation with architecture/program-flow/packet-flow visualizations.

Bundles screenshot assets under Code/assets and README embeds for setup view, key-exchange status, plaintext/encrypted packet captures, and Wireshark clear-vs-secure comparisons.

Co-authored-by: Tasnia <tasnia.7r@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Fix: dispatch next Slack item after rating, not after modal submit

When a reviewer clicks 'Skip' on the comment modal, Slack sends no
callback, so dispatchNextForReviewer was never called — the reviewer
got stuck with no more items. Now dispatch happens right after the
rating action completes, regardless of modal interaction.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add initial implementation of Bot Framework library

- Created pyproject.toml for project configuration and dependencies.
- Implemented core components including BotApplication, BotAuth, and TokenManager.
- Added HTTP client for handling requests to Bot Framework services.
- Developed conversation management features in ConversationClient.
- Introduced core activity models for handling messages and activities.
- Implemented middleware support for processing activities.
- Added user token management functionality with UserTokenClient.
- Created unit tests for BotApplication and CoreActivity functionalities.

* refactor: update Python directory structure to use 'python/packages/botas'

* feat: Implement Bot Framework middleware and client libraries

- Add bot-auth-middleware for validating Bot Framework Bearer tokens.
- Introduce bot-http-client for making authenticated HTTP requests to the Bot Framework REST API.
- Create conversation-client for managing conversations, sending activities, and retrieving members.
- Define core-activity types and utility functions for activity handling.
- Implement i-turn-middleware interface for cross-cutting concerns in activity processing.
- Develop user-token-client for managing user tokens and OAuth sign-in flows.
- Add logger utility for consistent logging across the library.
- Implement token-manager for acquiring Bot Framework access tokens using MSAL.
- Create tests for core-activity functionality.
- Add TypeScript configuration for the project.

* refactor: update folder structure and paths across documentation and project files

* refactor: reorder imports in bot_application.py and __init__.py for consistency

* refactor: reorganize dotnet project structure and migrate ChannelAccount and Conversation classes to CoreActivity namespace

* feat: add SVG logo for botas SDK

---------

commit.message:
redesign: Catppuccin Mocha + Linear-style minimalist UI

- Replace glassmorphism/glow/blur with flat Catppuccin Mocha palette
- Switch to Geist font family (body + mono)
- Redesign Layout: clean 240px sidebar, no decorative elements
- Redesign all 9 pages with generous whitespace and clear hierarchy
- Remove all side-stripe borders, gradient text, and decorative orbs
- Net -452 lines: cleaner, more maintainable code

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: storage policy for Jetson .engine upload via anon key

Migration 019 adds 3 policies on storage.objects for the 'models' bucket:
- INSERT: anon can upload to engines/* (new engine after conversion)
- UPDATE: anon can upsert engines/* (re-conversion overwrites)
- SELECT: anon can read engines/* (download on fresh setup)

All scoped to 'engines/' prefix only — does not affect model files
managed by admin dashboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: upload .engine files to Supabase Storage after TensorRT conversion

After converting ONNX → TensorRT, the .engine file is uploaded to
Supabase Storage as a device-specific backup. Storage path includes
hardware model and TensorRT version for clear identification:

  models/engines/{jetson-orin-nano}_trt{10.3}/{leaf_type}_student.engine

Device info auto-detected from /proc/device-tree/model and dpkg.
Upload is best-effort — failure doesn't block setup.

NOTE: .engine files are NOT portable across GPU architectures.
Each device must either convert ONNX locally or download an engine
built on identical hardware + TensorRT version.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: E2E pipeline binary + LoRA CLI integration + unpatchify_latent

- Add `src/bin/pipeline.rs`: full text→WAV pipeline (RF model + DACVAE decode)
- Add `InferenceBuilder::load_weights_with_adapter` (type-state LoRA entrypoint)
- Add `--adapter <dir>` flag to both `infer` and `pipeline` binaries
- Add `load_model_with_lora` to `weights.rs` (now called by both paths)
- Add `unpatchify_latent` to `model::speaker_encoder`, re-exported from lib
- Add `DacVaeCodec::sample_rate()` and `hop_length()` accessors
- Add `just pipeline` and `just pipeline-real` recipes to justfile
- Update `docs/feature-coverage.md`: LoRA ✅, E2E pipeline ✅, stale stubs cleaned

- introduce mobile pane switcher (problem/editor/result) and auto-focus result pane after run/test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(lint): cover locate_json_key None path and check_file malformed JSON

Add tests for two previously uncovered branches in the hook lint rules:

1. hook_legacy_event.rs line 101: the Err(_) arm in check_file() that
   silently skips malformed JSON was never exercised.

2. hook_legacy_event.rs / hook_unknown_event.rs: locate_json_key()
   returns None when the JSON key in the raw file is written using
   a Unicode escape (e.g. "St\u006fp" → "Stop"). serde_json resolves
   the escape when parsing, but locate_json_key searches the raw bytes
   for the literal key string and cannot find it — resulting in a
   diagnostic with line=None, col=None, end_col=None.

Both hook_legacy_event.rs and hook_unknown_event.rs now report 100%
branch coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(plugin): document direct Copilot install

Add the direct `copilot plugin install manmartgarc/negent:plugin` flow alongside the marketplace install steps and link the GitHub Copilot CLI plugin docs.

This gives Copilot users a copy-pasteable install command when they do not want to register the marketplace first, and keeps the CLI `auto` guidance aligned with GitHub's documented `OWNER/REPO:PATH` install syntax:
https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/plugins-finding-installing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add LightComponent for player dynamic light

Replace hardcoded player light in main.cpp with a proper LightComponent.

- Create LightComponent (h/cpp): holds a Light struct, getLight() returns
  the light with position synced to the owner GameObject
- Attach LightComponent to player in setupPlayer with warm white settings
  (color 1.0/0.95/0.8, intensity 0.6, radius 300, z 80)
- Register player light via getComponent<LightComponent>()->getLight()
  before draw calls in both render paths (editor and gameplay)
- Remove both duplicated hardcoded player light blocks
- Add LightComponent.cpp to CMakeLists.txt
- Update architecture.md with new component documentation
- Move completed Task 5 to CompletedTasks.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Open Graph and Twitter Cards metadata for social sharing

Add custom HTML template with Open Graph and Twitter Cards meta tags to
enable rich previews when sharing links on Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn,
and other social platforms.

Metadata includes:
- og:title, og:description, og:image
- twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image
- Site and creator attribution
